# core.PBOutput { #paleobeasts.core.PBOutput }

```python
core.PBOutput(
    time,
    state_variables,
    state_variable_names,
    diagnostic_variables,
    solution,
    run_name,
)
```

Container for the results of one call to ``PBModel.integrate()``.

``PBOutput`` carries the full trajectory produced by the solver and
exposes output-focused operations (noise addition, pyleoclim export,
time resampling).  Keeping these on the output rather than on the model
means a single model instance can produce multiple independent outputs
without them overwriting each other.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

| Name                 | Type                  | Description                                                                                                                                                                      |
|----------------------|-----------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| model_time           | ndarray of float      | Raw time axis as returned by the solver.  Never modified after construction; inspect this to understand the solver's actual grid.                                                |
| time                 | ndarray of float      | User-facing time axis.  Starts equal to ``model_time``; replaced by ``reframe_time_axis`` when a different grid is requested. ``state_variables`` is always aligned to ``time``. |
| state_variables      | structured ndarray    | Named state variable arrays, indexed by variable name.                                                                                                                           |
| state_variable_names | list of str           | Ordered list of state variable names.                                                                                                                                            |
| diagnostic_variables | dict of str → ndarray | Named diagnostic arrays.                                                                                                                                                         |
| solution             | object                | Raw solver output (retained for dense-output resampling via ``reframe_time_axis``).                                                                                              |
| run_name             | str                   | Label for this integration run.                                                                                                                                                  |

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [add_noise](#paleobeasts.core.PBOutput.add_noise) | Add externally provided noise to an output variable. |
| [get_series_by_name](#paleobeasts.core.PBOutput.get_series_by_name) | Return a variable's array and its storage location. |
| [reframe_time_axis](#paleobeasts.core.PBOutput.reframe_time_axis) | Resample state variables onto a new time axis. |
| [remove_noise](#paleobeasts.core.PBOutput.remove_noise) | Restore a variable to its pre-noise values. |
| [to_pyleo](#paleobeasts.core.PBOutput.to_pyleo) | Export one or more variables as pyleoclim Series objects. |

### add_noise { #paleobeasts.core.PBOutput.add_noise }

```python
core.PBOutput.add_noise(var_name, noise_ts)
```

Add externally provided noise to an output variable.

The unmodified values are saved on the first call so that
``remove_noise`` can restore the clean series.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name     | Type         | Description                                              | Default    |
|----------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| var_name | str          | Name of a state or diagnostic variable.                  | _required_ |
| noise_ts | array - like | Noise series with the same shape as the target variable. | _required_ |

### get_series_by_name { #paleobeasts.core.PBOutput.get_series_by_name }

```python
core.PBOutput.get_series_by_name(var_name)
```

Return a variable's array and its storage location.

State variables live in a structured numpy array; diagnostic
variables live in a dict.  Returning the location string lets
callers that write back (e.g. ``add_noise``) know which container
to update.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name     | Type                        | Description   |
|----------|-----------------------------|---------------|
| values   | ndarray of float            |               |
| location | {\'state\', \'diagnostic\'} |               |

### reframe_time_axis { #paleobeasts.core.PBOutput.reframe_time_axis }

```python
core.PBOutput.reframe_time_axis(t_eval)
```

Resample state variables onto a new time axis.

Uses the dense output from ``solve_ivp`` when available (accurate
polynomial interpolation); falls back to linear interpolation for
fixed-step solvers.

After this call, ``time`` is replaced by ``t_eval`` and
``state_variables`` is replaced by the interpolated values.
``model_time`` is never modified and always reflects the solver's
original grid.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type         | Description       | Default    |
|--------|--------------|-------------------|------------|
| t_eval | array - like | Target time axis. | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name     | Type                          | Description                              |
|----------|-------------------------------|------------------------------------------|
| reframed | structured ndarray or ndarray | Resampled state variables on ``t_eval``. |

### remove_noise { #paleobeasts.core.PBOutput.remove_noise }

```python
core.PBOutput.remove_noise(var_name)
```

Restore a variable to its pre-noise values.

Reverses ``add_noise`` by replacing the noisy array with the
clean copy saved on the first ``add_noise`` call.

### to_pyleo { #paleobeasts.core.PBOutput.to_pyleo }

```python
core.PBOutput.to_pyleo(var_names=None)
```

Export one or more variables as pyleoclim Series objects.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name      | Type               | Description                                           | Default   |
|-----------|--------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|-----------|
| var_names | str or list of str | Name(s) of state or diagnostic variable(s) to export. | `None`    |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                         | Description   |
|--------|----------------------------------------------|---------------|
|        | pyleoclim.Series or pyleoclim.MultipleSeries |               |
